chapter: Chapter 1: A Square and a Cube

questions.question: Explain how to determine whether a given number is a perfect square using prime factorisation.
questions.answer: Write the number as a product of its prime factors. If every prime factor appears in pairs, the number is a perfect square. For example, 144 = 2⁴ × 3², where all factors are in pairs, so it is a perfect square.

questions.question: Explain the method of finding the square root of a number using prime factorisation with an example.
questions.answer: First write the number as a product of its prime factors. Then group the factors into pairs. Take one factor from each pair to obtain the square root. For example, 196 = 2² × 7², so √196 = 14.

questions.question: Explain the method of finding cube roots using prime factorisation with an example.
questions.answer: Write the number as a product of its prime factors. Group the factors into sets of three identical factors. Take one factor from each group to obtain the cube root. For example, 512 = 2⁹, so ∛512 = 2³ = 8.

questions.question: Find the square root of 900 using prime factorisation.
questions.answer: 900 = 2² × 3² × 5². Grouping the factors gives √900 = 2 × 3 × 5 = 30.

questions.question: Find the cube root of 1728 using prime factorisation.
questions.answer: 1728 = 2⁶ × 3³. Grouping in triples gives ∛1728 = 2² × 3 = 12.

questions.question: Explain why the square of an odd number is always odd.
questions.answer: An odd number can be written as 2n + 1. Squaring it gives (2n + 1)² = 4n² + 4n + 1, which is also odd.

questions.question: Explain why the cube of an even number is always even.
questions.answer: An even number can be written as 2n. Its cube is (2n)³ = 8n³, which is divisible by 2, so it is even.

questions.question: Explain the difference between perfect squares and perfect cubes with examples.
questions.answer: A perfect square is obtained by multiplying a number by itself, while a perfect cube is obtained by multiplying a number by itself three times. For example, 16 = 4² and 27 = 3³.

questions.question: Explain how square numbers are related to the area of a square.
questions.answer: The area of a square is found by multiplying the side by itself. If the side length is a, then area = a², which is a square number.

questions.question: Explain how cube numbers relate to the volume of a cube.
questions.answer: The volume of a cube is found by multiplying the side length three times. If the side is a, then volume = a³.

questions.question: Find the square root of 784.
Find the cube root of 3375.
Explain why every perfect square has an even number of prime factors.
questions.answer: A perfect square is formed by multiplying a number by itself. Therefore each prime factor appears in pairs, giving an even number of factors.

questions.question: Explain why every perfect cube has prime factors in groups of three.
questions.answer: A perfect cube is formed by multiplying a number by itself three times, so each prime factor appears in multiples of three.

questions.question: Find the square root of 1024 using prime factorisation.
questions.answer: 1024 = 2¹⁰. Pairing the factors gives √1024 = 2⁵ = 32.

questions.question: Find the cube root of 2197.
Explain why 625 is a perfect square.
questions.answer: 625 = 25 × 25 = 5⁴, so it is a perfect square.

questions.question: Explain why 512 is a perfect cube.
Find the square root of 1600.
Find the cube root of 1000.
Explain why the square of a multiple of 5 is also a multiple of 5.
questions.answer: If a number is written as 5n, its square is (5n)² = 25n², which is divisible by 5.

questions.question: Explain why the cube of a multiple of 3 is also a multiple of 3.
questions.answer: If a number is written as 3n, then (3n)³ = 27n³, which is divisible by 3.

questions.question: Explain why the square of any natural number is positive.
questions.answer: Multiplying a number by itself always results in a positive number.

questions.question: Explain why the cube of a negative number is negative.
questions.answer: Multiplying a negative number three times results in a negative value.

questions.question: Explain the importance of square and cube numbers in mathematics.
questions.answer: Square numbers are used in area calculations and cube numbers are used in volume calculations. They are also important in algebra and number theory.
